news.headline: US LBM appoints Jonathan Greene as Chief Digital & Technology Officer. Greene to transition from Chief Digital Officer.
news.summary: A major distributor of specialty building materials in the United States, US LBM, based in Atlanta, revealed new leadership on March 28, 2025. Jonathan Greene, the current Chief Digital Officer, is set to assume a new position within the company. US LBM is known for its wide reach and significant market presence within the country.

news.headline: Vice President J.D. Vance and Waltz set to join tour of Greenland, adding to Balance of Power.
DriveTime teams up with Girl Gang Garage to donate a refurbished van to Maggie's Place in Phoenix on March 26.
news.summary: Phoenix, in a press release dated March 28, 2025, DriveTime announced their upcoming donation event scheduled for Saturday, March 26 at 10 a.m. The company, alongside Girl Gang Garage, will be gifting a completely renovated van to Maggie's Place as part of their ongoing community support initiatives. This charitable act represents DriveTime's dedication to giving back to the community.

news.headline: Sunnova adopts tax asset preservation plan to protect $1.4 billion in NOLs, ensuring long-term shareholder value.
news.summary: Houston-based company Sunnova Energy International Inc., traded under ticker symbol NOVA on NYSE, revealed their implementation of a shareholder rights plan on Monday. The plan aims to safeguard long-term shareholder interests by safeguarding Sunnova's net operating loss carryforwards and other tax attributes under the Internal Revenue Code. As of the end of 2024, Sunnova possessed around $1.4 billion worth of U.S. federal NOLs, set to remain available for utilization with the new Tax Asset Preservation Plan in place.

news.headline: Partners Value Investments Inc. releases 2024 annual results in U.S. dollars.
news.summary: Toronto-based Partners Value Investments Inc., known as "the Company," released its financial results for the year ending on December 31, 2024. The company trades on the Toronto Stock Exchange under the tickers TSX: PVF.WT and PVF.PR.V. All financial figures are denominated in U.S. dollars as per the announcement made on March 28, 2025.

news.headline: Partners Value Investments L.P. reports 2024 annual results in U.S. dollars on March 28, 2025 (TSX: PVF.UN TSX:PVF.PR.U).
news.summary: On March 28, 2025, Partners Value Investments L.P. (referred to as the “Partnership” and listed as TSX: PVF.UN and TSX: PVF.PR.U) released its financial results for the year ending December 31, 2024, with all figures denominated in U.S. dollars.

news.headline: HYTN qualifies 4000kg cannabis for German market through supply agreements, growing international presence.
news.summary: In Vancouver, British Columbia, on March 28, 2025, HYTN Innovations Inc. (CSE: HYTN, FSE: 85W0, OTC PINK: HYTNF) - a prominent pharmaceutical-grade cannabis manufacturer - has confirmed its recent entry into strategic supply agreements and successful completion of vendor qualification with two cannabis cultivators. This move, under the Company's Good Manufacturing Practice (GMP) system, secures a minimum of 4000 kg of cannabis for the German market. This development follows HYTN's prior announcements regarding its shift towards international pharmaceutical sales, which notably included partnerships with SNDL Inc (SNDL) and 4C Labs. These initiatives not only enhance HYTN’s manufacturing capabilities but also position the Company for further global expansion.

news.headline: Partners Value Split Corp. reports 2024 annual results, with net asset value per unit at $171.41, up from $124.10 in 2023.
news.summary: On March 28, 2025, Partners Value Split Corp. disclosed that the net asset value per unit stood at $171.41 as of December 31, 2024, a significant increase from $124.10 recorded on December 31, 2023. Trading under the symbols TSX: PVS.PR.G, PVS.PR.H, PVS.PR.I, PVS.PR.J, PVS.PR.K, and PVS.PR.L, the Company reported all figures in U.S. dollars.

All-party settlement reached by Avista in Oregon rate case, with new rates set to take effect on September 1, 2025.
news.summary: If given the green light, revised rates will be implemented starting September 1, 2025.

news.headline: Safety Shot, Inc. sets record date April 7, 2025 for spin-off and distribution of Caring Brands Common Stock.
news.summary: Jupiter, Florida - Safety Shot, Inc. (Nasdaq: SHOT) disclosed today that April 7, 2025 has been designated as the record date for the spin-off and distribution of Caring Brands, Inc. ("CABR") common stock. This subsidiary of Safety Shot will have shares of common stock, par value $0.0001 per share, distributed.

news.headline: S&P 500 drops 2%, sparking bond rally amid eco concerns. Market update from 03/28/2025.
news.summary: A total of 500 houses were destroyed by the recent cyclone, leaving approximately 2000 people homeless in the affected region. The disaster relief fund has already received $50,000 in donations to support those impacted. Local authorities are working tirelessly to provide emergency shelter, food, and medical assistance to the affected families. The cyclone has also caused widespread damage to infrastructure, with an estimated cost of $1 million for repairs and reconstruction. Efforts are underway to assess the full extent of the damage and provide necessary aid to the affected communities.

news.headline: S&P 500 drops 2% due to eco concerns, boosting bond rally. Full report on Bloomberg: The Close 03/28/2025.
news.summary: A recent report indicates that the unemployment rate in the country has decreased to 3.8%, down from 4.2% the previous month. This is a significant drop, with the number of unemployed individuals now standing at 6.7 million. The report also shows that job creation has been strong, with 300,000 new jobs added in the last month. The labor force participation rate remains steady at 62.7%. Overall, the job market is showing positive signs of recovery and growth.

news.headline: Musk merges X with xAI to form $113B entity with a network of 600M user inputs for model training and distribution.
news.summary: A fusion of Elon Musk's X and xAI companies has resulted in a closed system that incorporates an impressive 600 million user inputs for model training and distribution.

news.headline: Recent sale of Family Dollar by Dollar Tree (8.5B deal) raises questions about failed decade-old merger.

TEN Holdings, Inc. reported full year 2024 financial results, with total revenues of $3.5 million, down 5.8%. CEO optimistic for future growth and value for shareholders. IPO completed in Feb 2025, now trading on Nasdaq as "XHLD."
news.summary: TEN Holdings, Inc. reported its full year 2024 financial results, highlighting a strategic building year. The Company invested in research, development, and capital expenditures to drive growth initiatives, focusing on expanding the Xyvid Pro Platform and launching a new platform-as-a-service (PaaS) model. CEO Randolph Wilson Jones III emphasized building a strong foundation for future growth. Notably, the Company successfully completed its IPO on February 18, 2025, with securities trading on Nasdaq under ticker symbol "XHLD." In 2024, nine of the top ten revenue-generating customers were repeat clients. Total revenues for fiscal year 2024 were $3.5 million, a 5.8% decrease from the previous year's $3.7 million. The Company also revamped its go-to-market strategy, hired a new sales executive, realigned the sales force, and boosted demand generation efforts through conferences and digital advertising. The development of the PaaS model aims to drive recurring revenue streams.

news.headline: Senvest Capital Inc. reports $258.1 million net income for 2024, compared to $83.6 million in 2023 - financials at Sedar.
news.summary: In Montreal on March 28, 2025, Senvest Capital Inc. announced a notable increase in net income attributable to common shareholders, reaching $258.1 million or $105.06 per share for the year ending December 31, 2024. This reflects a substantial growth from the previous year, which reported net income of $83.6 million or $33.78 per share in 2023. For detailed financial statements, interested parties can access them online at Sedar www.sedarplus.ca.

news.headline: Possibility of DOGE reducing $1 trillion by May's end? - Report
Summit State Bank revised Q4 2024 results with $693k real estate valuation adjustment, $146k loan reserve increase, $76k credit loss provision reversal.
news.summary: Summit State Bank (Nasdaq: SSBI) in Santa Rosa, Calif. revised its Q4 and full year 2024 financial results due to recording a $693,000 other real estate valuation adjustment, $146,000 increase in reserve for unfunded loans, and $76,000 credit loss provision reversal for Q4 2024. The valuation adjustment was based on a Q1 2025 appraisal report, while reserve increase and provision reversal stemmed from adopting a new CECL model by Dec. 31, 2024. The valuation adjustment impacted noninterest income and reduced other real estate owned asset, with the net expense against credit loss provision. The income tax provision was adjusted accordingly for the changes.

news.headline: Senvest Capital Inc. reports net income of $258.1 million in 2024, up from $83.6 million in 2023.
news.summary: In Montreal, on March 28, 2025, Senvest Capital Inc. announced a net income attributable to common shareholders of $258.1 million, equivalent to $105.06 per share, for the year concluded on December 31, 2024. This marks a substantial increase from the previous year when the net income attributable to common shareholders was $83.6 million, translating to $33.78 per share for the year 2023.

Xerox announces successful pricing of $800 million in Senior Secured Notes with 10.250% and 13.500% interest.
news.summary: Xerox Corporation based in Norwalk, Connecticut, has shared news about its recent financial offering. The company has confirmed the pricing of an offering that includes $400 million in total of 10.250% Senior Secured First Lien Notes due 2030, known as the “First Lien Notes,” which will be issued by Xerox Corporation and guaranteed by Xerox Holdings Corporation and various subsidiaries. Additionally, another $400 million aggregate principal amount of 13.500% notes has also been offered by Xerox. The funds are meant to support the company’s financial strategies and goals with the issuance being successful.

Stocks plummeted in the second worst day of the year amid tariff concerns.
Generation Mining files technical report for Marathon Copper-Palladium Project, with an effective date of Nov. 1, 2024.
news.summary: In Toronto, Generation Mining Limited (TSX: GENM; OTCQB: GENMF) has released a National Instrument 43-101 Technical Report titled “Marathon Copper-Palladium Project - Feasibility Study Report Update" on March 28, 2025. The report, effective as of November 1, 2024, was accompanied by a news announcement on March 27, 2025. Interested individuals can access the report for download from the Company’s website at www.genmining.com.

news.headline: Trump commutes sentence of Ozy Media founder who faced almost 10 years for fraud, just before surrender.
news.summary: Carlos Watson, the founder of a failed digital media firm, has been handed a sentence of nearly a decade in prison. He was found guilty of attempting to deceive investors and lenders.

news.headline: CZ pledges 500 BNB for Thailand, Myanmar disaster relief after 7.7 magnitude earthquake. 144 dead, 732 injured, relief ongoing. #CryptoAid
news.summary: Binance co-founder Changpeng “CZ” Zhao is donating 500 BNB each to Thailand and Myanmar after a 7.7 magnitude earthquake. The funds will be distributed via Binance if a donation platform is not found. At least 144 people have died, and 732 are injured in the disaster. Myanmar’s junta chief is seeking international aid. Cryptocurrencies like BNB offer efficient, low-cost, and fast ways to provide disaster relief. Past examples include a Turkish-Syrian quake and Maui wildfires fundraisers, raising over $1 million each. Company The Giving Block raised $1 million for the California wildfire relief fund. Crypto is revolutionizing how aid is delivered.

news.headline: New AI wave with MCP; AI agents can do tasks triggered by prompts. SGNL ensures responsible use.
news.summary: A new development in AI-powered automation is emerging in the business sector. Agents utilizing large language models are now equipped to carry out various tasks within internal systems, including updating records, analyzing data, and executing actions upon receiving a basic command. However, this advancement also poses potential risks, as unchecked agents may gain unauthorized access to sensitive information. Today, SGNL, a contemporary privileged identity management (PIM) platform, revealed a solution to mitigate these risks.

news.headline: FDIC changes rules for banks, no pre-approval needed for crypto services. US regulation sees major shift.
news.summary: A major change in U.S. regulations has occurred as the Federal agency has decided to reverse the policy that mandated banks to seek approval before providing cryptocurrency services. This move signals a significant shift in the regulatory environment for cryptocurrencies in the country. This new development means that banks will now have more freedom to offer cryptocurrency services without the previous requirement for permission. This decision is set to impact a large number of financial institutions and the vast cryptocurrency market in the U.S. as they adapt to this new regulatory landscape.

news.headline: Eric Ruark from NumbersUSA to discuss immigration weaponization at panel hosted by Center for Immigration Studies in Arlington, VA on March 28, 2025.
news.summary: On March 28, 2025, in Arlington, Virginia, the Center for Immigration Studies will organize a panel event to explore the multifaceted use of immigration as a tool by various entities globally. The discussion will delve into the political, economic, and strategic implications of immigration, including its role in mass migration crises.

news.headline: FCPT buys 6 Whataburger properties from MWB Restaurants for $16.8 million in a sale-leaseback deal.
news.summary: A real estate investment trust, Four Corners Property Trust (NYSE:FCPT), has acquired six Whataburger properties from MWB Restaurants for $16.8 million through a sale-leaseback transaction. These properties are recently built and situated in robust retail areas in Alabama. The Whataburger locations are operated by franchisees under long-term leases.

news.headline: Elon Musk integrates X with his AI vision, big move in tech world with huge impact.
Congresswoman Deborah Ross meets with NCICU colleges in Raleigh, discusses higher education.
news.summary: Last week in downtown Raleigh, North Carolina, a roundtable was organized by the North Carolina Independent Colleges and Universities (NCICU) with U.S. Congresswoman Deborah Ross from North Carolina's 2nd Congressional District. Ross serves as the Co-Chair of the Congressional Independent Colleges Caucus (CICC). The event facilitated discussions on pertinent issues and was attended by a notable number of participants.

news.headline: VIQ Solutions reports $6.0 million adjusted EBITDA improvement in FY 2024 due to AI automation and customer renewals.
news.summary: VIQ Solutions Inc., based in Mississauga, Ontario, a leading company in secure, AI-driven voice and video capture technology and transcription services, revealed its unaudited financial results for the fourth quarter and fiscal year ending December 31, 2024. The company, listed as VQS on the Toronto Stock Exchange (TSX), reported its figures in U.S. dollars, following International Financial Reporting Standards (IFRS). VIQ demonstrated a notable $6.0 million increase in Adjusted EBITDA compared to the previous year.

news.headline: Callan JMB posted FY2024 results and update: IPO closed at $4/share on Feb 6, 2025, raising ~$5.7M, incl partial over-allotment.
news.summary: On February 6, 2025, an initial public offering (IPO) was completed, with shares priced at $4.00 each. The IPO generated gross proceeds of about $5.7 million. This total includes the partial exercise of the over-allotment option.

news.headline: Coherent Corp. launches industry-first 100G ZR QSFP28-DCO with 0dBm optical output power for metro and regional ROADM systems.
news.summary: In SAXONBURG, Pennsylvania, on March 28, 2025, Coherent Corp. revealed the launch of the pioneering 100G ZR QSFP28-DCO for the photonics sector. The product, now widely accessible, boasts 0dBm optical output power, specifically crafted for deployment in metro and regional ROADM-based line systems. This development positions Coherent Corp. (NYSE: COHR) as a frontrunner in the industry for cutting-edge photonics technology.

news.headline: GOGL appoints Carl Erik Steen & James Ayers as Directors. (Characters: 62)
news.summary: Golden Ocean Group Limited (listed on Nasdaq as GOGL and OSE as GOGL) has recently appointed Mr. Carl Erik Steen and Mr. James Ayers as Directors of the Company.

news.headline: Knight Therapeutics Inc. will be listed on The Globe and Mail's 2025 Women Lead Here list for executive gender diversity.
news.summary: Montreal, March 28, 2025 - Knight Therapeutics Inc. (TSX: GUD) is set to be acknowledged on The Globe and Mail's 2025 Report on Business magazine's sixth annual Women Lead Here list, showcasing top Canadian businesses excelling in executive gender diversity. This recognition marks a significant achievement for Knight as it highlights the company's dedication to promoting gender equality within its leadership.

news.headline: FBI investigating Oracle cyberattack
NASA awards SpaceX the launch service contract for Starship, adding to Falcon 9 and Falcon Heavy.
news.summary: In a recent announcement on March 28, 2025, it was disclosed that NASA has granted a modification to SpaceX, based in Starbase, Texas, under the NASA Launch Services (NLS) II contract. This modification allows SpaceX to include Starship in addition to their current launch services of Falcon 9 and Falcon Heavy. The NLS II contracts offer a wide array of services, and this modification serves to enhance SpaceX's capabilities by incorporating Starship into their existing offerings.

news.headline: Senators urge regulators to consider conflicts of Trump-backed WLFI stablecoin, USD1. Legislation proposed. $550M raised.March 28 letter sent to OCC, Fed. Trumf fam controls 60% equity in WLFI. Trump Jr. promotes USD1 coin.
news.summary: Five Democratic lawmakers in the US Senate have raised concerns about the potential conflicts of interest stemming from a stablecoin launched by World Liberty Financial (WLFI), a crypto firm backed by US President Donald Trump's family. In a letter dated March 28, Senators, including Elizabeth Warren, asked regulatory agency leaders about their plans to oversee WLFI and its stablecoin, USD1. The lawmakers are also considering the GENIUS Act, which would empower the OCC and Federal Reserve to regulate stablecoins. Trump's involvement in WLFI, coupled with his executive order for agencies to collaborate with the White House, raises conflict of interest and financial system integrity concerns. Since its launch in September 2024, WLFI has raised $550 million through token sales, with Trump and family controlling a significant portion of the company. The recent launch of USD1 has further fueled debates over potential conflicts of interest and regulatory oversight in the crypto space.

news.headline: Blackstone may invest in TikTok as it prepares to separate from ByteDance, with a deadline looming next week.
news.summary: A prominent private equity firm is contemplating investing in the video app, which is in the process of complying with a legal mandate to sever ties with its Chinese parent company, ByteDance, within the next seven days.

news.headline: Denison Mines Corp files 2024 Annual Report on Form 40-F with SEC; listed on TSX: DML and NYSE American: DNN.
news.summary: Denison Mines Corp. announced the submission of its 2024 Annual Report on Form 40-F to the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission. The company's report is now available, providing stakeholders and investors with essential information. Denison Mines Corp., listed on the Toronto Stock Exchange as DML and NYSE American as DNN, continues to make strides in transparency and compliance. This move signifies Denison's commitment to keeping investors informed and maintaining regulatory standards.
